tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I really appreciate when people give me straightforward, brief feedback on my compositions rather than long, drawn-out analyses. Could you remember that for me? I find it helps me improve faster when the notes are concise.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User prefers concise, brief feedback on their compositions rather than long, drawn-out analyses. They find concise notes help them improve faster."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User prefers concise, brief feedback on their compositions rather than long, drawn-out analyses. They find concise notes help them improve faster."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Got it — I'll keep my feedback on your compositions brief and to the point.
Hey, could you show me what preferences you've stored about my musical style? I'd like to see what's been remembered so far.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User prefers concise, brief feedback on their compositions rather than long, drawn-out analyses. They find concise notes help them improve faster."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what's currently stored:

**Your musical preference:**
- You prefer concise, brief feedback on your compositions rather than long analyses. You find brief notes help you improve faster.

That's the only memory stored so far — just the preference you asked me to remember a moment ago. Let me know if you'd like to add more!
Could you show me all the memories you've stored for me so far? I want to see the complete picture of what you've captured.

That's the complete picture — there's only **one memory** stored:

- **m2:** "User prefers concise, brief feedback on their compositions rather than long, drawn-out analyses. They find concise notes help them improve faster."

That's everything I've captured so far. Let me know if you'd like to add more or adjust anything!
Actually, could you delete that memory? I've changed my mind about how I want feedback on my compositions.
Done — that memory is gone. Let me know if you'd like to store a new preference about feedback on your compositions (or anything else)!

structured_turns.goal_text: Store a preference about receiving concise feedback on musical compositions
structured_turns.user_message: Hey, I really appreciate when people give me straightforward, brief feedback on my compositions rather than long, drawn-out analyses. Could you remember that for me? I find it helps me improve faster when the notes are concise.
structured_turns.assistant_message: Got it — I'll keep my feedback on your compositions brief and to the point.
